prompt: Write a 150-word short story about a lighthouse keeper who discovers something unexpected in the fog. Do not use dialogue.
response:
The lighthouse beam cut through the thick, gray fog like a knife, sweeping across the turbulent waters. Jonas had been keeper of this isolated lighthouse for twenty-three years, and he thought he knew every sound, every rhythm of the sea. But today was different.

As the mist swirled around the rocky coastline, something moved in the periphery of his vision. At first, he dismissed it as a trick of the light. But then he saw it clearly: a figure, pale and translucent, standing on the jagged rocks below.

The figure was neither human nor completely solid, seeming to shimmer and dissolve with each pass of the lighthouse lamp. Jonas gripped the railing, his weathered hands trembling. He knew the stories sailors whispered about these waters, tales of lost souls and maritime ghosts.

The apparition turned, meeting his gaze with eyes that held centuries of untold stories, and then slowly vanished into the consuming whiteness of the fog.
